title: <name> and the Homework Helper Gone Bananas

pages.text: <name> loved science and inventing things. One Saturday afternoon, he decided to build something truly amazing — a robot that could help with homework! He gathered spare wires, blinking lights, and an old tablet for a face. “I’ll call you Homi the Homework Helper!” <name> said proudly.
When he pressed the green button, the robot blinked its bright eyes and chirped, “Hello, student <name>! Ready to learn?”

Everything started great — until Homi began to misunderstand the instructions. When <name> said, “Draw the number 8,” the robot proudly drew eight bananas across the page! When he asked for spelling help, Homi began singing poems about pencils in a funny robotic voice.
<name> laughed so hard that he almost forgot he was supposed to be fixing it. But then — splurt! — Homi’s ink pen burst, flooding the desk with sparkly blue ink that shimmered like starlight.

“Oh no, Homi! That’s too much sparkle!” said <name>. But instead of panicking, he took a deep breath. He used a small screwdriver to open Homi’s back panel and adjusted a few glowing circuits. Then he typed in a new command: “Understand, don’t guess!”
Homi blinked again and beeped softly. This time, when <name> asked for 2 + 3, the robot drew a perfect “5” — with only one banana sticker beside it for fun.
Together, they cleaned up the glittery mess, laughing the whole time. <name> realized that even when things go a little bananas, creativity and patience can fix any problem.
That night, <name> and Homi finished the homework together — one number, one rhyme, and one giggle at a time.
